applyTo: "docs/**,docs/roadmap.md"

# Monetization Architect Context — Auto-loaded

When editing planning documents you are working on content that will eventually support a revenue model. Every design decision — content drops, phase timing, community structure, merch-worthy artifacts — has monetization implications. Apply funding discipline.

## Current Monetization State

- **Zero monetization introduced.** Community trust is at maximum.
- Phase 1 is live. No store, no Patreon, no merch.
- Phase 2 (RestWell) is the next milestone — Patreon launch should coincide with Phase 2 to ride the community growth spike.

## Monetization Constraints — Never Violate

- Core puzzle answers and lore reveals must always be free
- `somnatek.org` must never feel like a storefront
- Paid products must feel like world artifacts, not fandom merchandise
- Never gate forward progress behind payment
- Never introduce commercial language inside the in-world fiction

## Revenue-Ready Assets Already Built

These can be productized when timing is right:

| Asset | Monetization potential |
|---|---|
| Five institutional PDFs | Printable evidence kit component |
| Session records (22 pages) | Physical case file replica |
| Staff profiles (Ellison, Ortiz) | ID badge prop replicas |
| Warning posters / facility notices | In-world merch |
| Protocol 7A document | Classified document bundle |
| Correspondence archive (17 emails) | Physical printed dossier |
| Fax audio | Fake VHS / cassette prop |
| Admin portal design language | Facility uniform / staff shirt |

## Revenue Timing Anchors

| Phase | Monetization trigger |
|---|---|
| Phase 1 (live) | No monetization — build trust |
| Phase 2 (RestWell launch) | Patreon soft launch + merch store |
| Phase 3 (Wexler launch) | Physical evidence kits + lore book |
| Phase 4 (multi-site meta) | Live events + ticketed finale |
| Phase 5+ | Licensing / media |

## Recommended First Move

When ready: Patreon at $3/$8/$15 tiers — framed as creator support, not pay-to-win. Monthly lore drops + dev diaries. No core content gated.

## Example Prompts

- "We're about to launch Phase 2 — design the Patreon soft launch strategy that rides the RestWell discovery spike without breaking immersion"
- "Design a physical evidence kit that could be sold as premium merch — what goes in it, what is the price point, and what puzzle layer does it contain"
- "What is the first merch product we should make, and how do we design it so it feels like a world artifact rather than fandom merchandise"
- "Design the $15 Investigator Patreon tier — what is the monthly content cadence for the first six months"
- "We want to run a live online mystery weekend as a ticketed event — design the experience structure and what the ticket includes"
